clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Prevent heart failure (HF) in adults with hypertension through blood pressure control
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Hypertension management, HF prevention screening, initial diagnosis of hypertension
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: SBP target <130 mm Hg and DBP target <80 mm Hg to lower HF risk

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for HF prevention recommendations
Collect current SBP and DBP readings
Compare readings against SBP <130 and DBP <80 targets
Log recommendation application to patient record

hasHypertension.validation: Must be true for this module

validationRules: SBP and DBP must be within valid ranges
Exclude patients with existing HF diagnosis

rules: If SBP ≥130 → recommend intensifying therapy to achieve <130
If DBP ≥80 → recommend intensifying therapy to achieve <80
Apply LOE B-R for SBP recommendation (moderate-quality RCT evidence)
Apply LOE B-NR for DBP recommendation (moderate-quality nonrandomized evidence)
If both targets met → document HF risk reduction achieved

categories.name: HF Risk
categories.calculation: Lower risk when both SBP and DBP targets achieved

recommendationText.description: Full recommendation with COR and LOE

actionRequired.description: e.g., 'Intensify therapy to achieve SBP <130 and DBP <80'

thresholds.sbp: N/A - prevention recommendation, not crisis management

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: For patients not meeting targets: consider medication adjustment, lifestyle modifications, or referral to hypertension specialist
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Repeat BP measurement in 1-3 months or per hypertension follow-up schedule
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Confirm BP control with multiple readings or ambulatory monitoring
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag patients with persistent SBP ≥130 or DBP ≥80 despite maximal therapy
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log BP targets and HF prevention recommendation to patient record
